preamble: The hardening of [h] to [b] is a notable feature of certain language-wide morphological patterns, where a weakened glottal sound reverts to a stronger labial ancestor.

examples.note: Rendaku (sequential voicing): in compounds, initial /h/ of second element → [b]. Historical path: *p → [ɸ] → [h] → Rendaku [b].
Same Rendaku h→b pattern; h reflects historical *p (*pune > ɸune > hune).
